name: poor-economics
description: Banerjee and Duflo's Poor Economics — a Nobel Prize-winning development economics toolkit using randomized controlled trials to test what works in fighting poverty: education, healthcare, microfinance, savings, governance, and the everyday decisions of the poor.
useCase: Understand evidence-based development economics — what actually works in fighting poverty, tested through randomized controlled trials.
knownRisks: RCTs test narrow questions — the book does not address systemic issues like global inequality, trade policy, or climate change
The case for small, targeted interventions may underestimate the need for structural change
Some findings from specific contexts may not generalize globally
